summary: Indian-Canadian-American author. Known for the novel Jasmine and the story 'The Management of Grief' (inspired by Air India Flight 182 bombing). Themes of displacement and immigration.

works.name: Jasmine
works.description: Novel about an Indian woman who immigrates to America after husband's death; lives in Iowa with Bud Ripplemayer.

clues.clue: Title character kills ship captain Half-Face; settles in Iowa with wheelchair-bound banker Bud Ripplemayer
examples: kills the ship captain Half-Face before settling down in Iowa with the wheelchair-bound banker Bud Ripplemayer

clues.clue: Expanded from a story in The Middleman and Other Stories
examples: expanded a story from her collection The Middleman and Other Stories

clues.clue: Protagonist changes names (Jyoti, Jasmine, etc.) as she moves through life
examples: at first called Jyoti, but later changes names as she moves through her life

clues.clue: Adopted child Du; Darrel Lutz's refused loan applications
examples: Bud Ripplemayer who repeatedly refuses Darrel Lutz's loan applications

works.name: 'The Management of Grief'
works.description: Inspired by Air India Flight 182 bombing. Narrator Shaila helps social worker Judith Templeton.

clues.clue: Inspired by Air India Flight 182 bombing; Shaila's husband and sons killed
examples: the Air India Flight 182 bombing, which inspired her story 'The Management of Grief'
a woman wades into a bay in Ireland

clues.clue: Social worker Judith Templeton; narrator thinks 'I think I can only envy her' about Kusum
examples: 'I think I can only envy her' when thinking of Kusum

works.name: Other Works
works.description: The Tiger's Daughter, Wife, The Holder of the World, Darkness, The Middleman.

clues.clue: Wife — protagonist stabs husband seven times; essay 'An Invisible Woman'
examples: the protagonist of one of her novels who stabs her husband seven times

clues.clue: The Holder of the World — Beigh Masters uses VR to travel to Mughal era; Hannah Eaton becomes concubine
examples: Beigh Masters uses a virtual reality program to travel back in time

clues.clue: The Tiger's Daughter — Calcutta native Tara returns to India
examples: a Calcutta native named Tara who returns to India

clues.clue: Co-wrote The Sorrow and the Terror with Clark Blaise about Air India bombing
examples: wrote the book The Sorrow and the Terror with Clark Blaise

comprehensive_summary: Bharati Mukherjee is an Indian-Canadian-American author focused on displacement and immigration. Jasmine follows an Indian woman who kills Half-Face, immigrates to America, and settles in Iowa with Bud Ripplemayer. 'The Management of Grief' is inspired by the Air India Flight 182 bombing. Other works include Wife, The Tiger's Daughter, and The Holder of the World.

cards.front: Novel: Indian woman kills ship captain Half-Face
Novel: settles in Iowa with wheelchair-bound banker Bud Ripplemayer
Short Story: inspired by Air India Flight 182 bombing
Short Story: narrator Shaila helps social worker Judith Templeton with bereaved families
